title: error copying layer and metadata: can't write to an unnamed image
body:
building simple dockerfile I can get an error as seen below: 

```
[vagrant@vanilla-f26atomic hugo]$ cat Dockerfile 
FROM       registry.fedoraproject.org/fedora:rawhide

# Install hugo from copr
RUN curl -L https://copr.fedorainfracloud.org/coprs/dustymabe/hugo/repo/fedora-rawhide/dustymabe-hugo-fedora-rawhide.repo > /etc/yum.repos.d/dustymabe-hugo-fedora-rawhide.repo
RUN dnf install -y hugo && dnf clean all
[vagrant@vanilla-f26atomic hugo]$ sudo buildah bud .
STEP 1: FROM registry.fedoraproject.org/fedora:rawhide
STEP 2: RUN curl -L https://copr.fedorainfracloud.org/coprs/dustymabe/hugo/repo/fedora-rawhide/dustymabe-hugo-fedora-rawhide.repo > /etc/yum.repos.d/dustymabe-hugo-fedora-rawhide.repo
```

comments.body:

```
 cat Dockerfile 
FROM       registry.fedoraproject.org/fedora:rawhide

# Install hugo from copr
RUN curl -L https://copr.fedorainfracloud.org/coprs/dustymabe/hugo/repo/fedora-rawhide/dustymabe-hugo-fedora-rawhide.repo > /etc/yum.repos.d/dustymabe-hugo-fedora-rawhide.repo
RUN dnf install -y hugo && dnf clean all
```

# rpm -q buildah
buildah-0.3-2.gitb9b2a8a.fc26.x86_64
This should have been fixed by #150, which was after 0.1 but before 0.2.
In the meantime, you shouldn't hit the error if you specify a name to assign to the built image, using the `-t` flag.
buildah-0.2 should be available in updates-testing.
